summary: Build and operate large-scale, AWS-native systems for live sports streaming at global scale — from ML-driven event monitoring to developer self-service tooling.
highlights: Built a scalable VLM-based recognition system for automated event monitoring, deployed across the Prime Video Sports catalog and stress-tested live during Black Friday Football.
Drove a cost-optimization effort that identified and reclaimed over $350K in underutilized AWS resources in the first year.
Designed and shipped a self-service portal for image slates, cutting manual onboarding from two developer-days to none.
Championed AI-assisted log diagnosis for internal logs, enabling self-service root-causing for other teams.

summary: Built the internal developer platform — identity, observability, and CI/CD — and led the on-prem to AWS migration for engineering teams firm-wide.
highlights: Architected the firm's transition to Okta with in-house abstractions for other developers, reducing onboarding from several weeks to two days.
Onboarded Datadog and built migration tooling from legacy monitoring platforms, cutting mean-time-to-root-cause from 9 days to 2.
Created CI/CD pipelines that made full Okta and Datadog adoption a one-commit change for most applications in the firm.
Drove planning and execution of the on-premise to AWS migration, architecting bespoke solutions for internal teams as needed.
